major_assignments.description: Design a complete monetization system for a free-to-play game of your choosing. The system must be profitable (demonstrated through a revenue projection model), ethical (passing a regulatory compliance audit across four jurisdictions), and player-friendly (with every purchase opportunity justified). Include a competitive analysis and a written ethical evaluation of your own design.
objectives: Design a monetization system that balances revenue generation with player respect and autonomy
Build a revenue projection model demonstrating financial viability with realistic assumptions
Conduct a regulatory compliance audit covering major global jurisdictions
Critically evaluate your own design against dark pattern checklists and ethical frameworks
deliverables: Monetization design document specifying all purchase opportunities, prices, mechanics, and currency systems
Revenue projection model with sensitivity analysis for retention, conversion rate, and ARPDAU assumptions
Regulatory compliance audit covering GDPR, COPPA, Belgium, China, and at least one additional jurisdiction
Ethical analysis essay (1,500-2,000 words) evaluating your design against dark pattern checklist and ethical frameworks
Competitive analysis comparing your monetization to 3 existing games in the same genre with specific details

criteria: All purchase opportunities specified with prices, value propositions, and player benefit
System creates genuine value at multiple spending tiers including completely free play
No pay-to-win mechanics in competitive contexts and no exploitative dark patterns
Economy design includes source-sink balance analysis and inflation prevention
Model uses realistic assumptions grounded in published industry benchmarks
Sensitivity analysis identifies which variables most affect profitability
Break-even timeline is clearly identified with scenario modeling (best, expected, worst case)
Model accounts for seasonal variation and player lifecycle revenue curves
Regulatory audit is thorough across all specified jurisdictions with specific compliance measures
Ethical analysis honestly identifies any tension points in the design rather than rubber-stamping
Dark patterns are explicitly addressed: each either avoided or justified with full transparency
Age-appropriate design and parental control considerations are included
Three comparable games analyzed with specific monetization mechanics, pricing, and revenue data
Comparison identifies what your design does differently and provides evidence-based reasoning
Strengths and weaknesses of competing approaches are honestly assessed
Market positioning of your design relative to competitors is clearly articulated
Complete and viable monetization system with detailed specification and economy design
Revenue model demonstrates financial sustainability with realistic, benchmarked assumptions
Regulatory compliance and ethical analysis are thorough, honest, and actionable
Competitive analysis provides context and evidence-based justification for design choices

major_assignments.title: Live Game Analytics Dashboard and Recommendation Engine
major_assignments.description: Build a comprehensive analytics dashboard and recommendation system for a simulated live game. The dashboard must display real-time KPIs, retention cohorts, economy health, and revenue metrics. The recommendation engine must use player behavior data to predict churn and suggest personalized re-engagement strategies. Present your system with a professional stakeholder report.
objectives: Build an interactive analytics dashboard that enables data-driven live operations decisions
Implement a machine learning model that predicts player churn from behavioral signals
Design a recommendation system that suggests personalized re-engagement interventions
Present the complete system to stakeholders with clear documentation and actionable insights
deliverables: Interactive dashboard displaying KPIs, retention cohorts, economy metrics, and revenue trends
Churn prediction model with documented feature engineering, training process, and evaluation metrics
Re-engagement recommendation system that maps predicted churn risk to specific intervention strategies
Stakeholder presentation (15-20 slides) summarizing the system, key findings, and recommended actions
Technical documentation covering system architecture, model methodology, and maintenance guide (4-5 pages)

criteria: Dashboard displays all critical KPIs with clear, accurate visualizations
Retention cohort analysis is interactive with filtering by date range and acquisition source
Economy health metrics track source-sink balance and inflation indicators
Alert thresholds are configured for anomaly detection on key metrics
Feature engineering demonstrates understanding of behavioral signals that predict churn
Model is trained with appropriate handling of class imbalance
Evaluation uses precision, recall, F1, and AUC-ROC rather than accuracy alone
Model performance is honestly assessed with limitations acknowledged
Recommendations are personalized based on player segment and predicted risk level
Intervention strategies are specific and actionable (not generic 'send a notification')
Ethical guardrails prevent exploitative recommendations for vulnerable player segments
System documents expected impact of each intervention type
Stakeholder presentation is clear, professional, and actionable
Technical documentation enables another analyst to maintain and extend the system
Key findings are supported by data and honestly acknowledge uncertainty
Recommendations are prioritized by expected impact and implementation difficulty
Comprehensive, interactive dashboard suitable for live game operations
Well-evaluated churn prediction model with honest performance assessment
Ethical recommendation system with personalized, actionable interventions
Professional stakeholder presentation with thorough technical documentation

weekly_quizzes.description: Five quizzes assessing analytics concepts, statistical methods, monetization knowledge, and ethical reasoning

lab_assignments.description: Hands-on analytics exercises using real and simulated player datasets with Python analysis

major_assignments.description: Three comprehensive projects: analytics investigation, monetization design, and live dashboard system

midterm_examination.description: Mixed format exam covering analytics methodology, monetization design, ethical frameworks, and data interpretation

final_project.description: Capstone dashboard and recommendation system demonstrating integrated analytics competency

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for data analysis lab sessions and guest speaker presentations from industry analytics professionals. More than 2 unexcused absences result in a half-letter grade reduction.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments are penalized 10% per day. The analytics report has a hard deadline because the dataset represents a time-sensitive business decision. No late submissions accepted for the analytics report without prior arrangement.
course_policies.collaboration: Data analysis labs may be completed in pairs. All three major assignments must be completed individually. Sharing analysis code between students is prohibited, but discussing analytical approaches is encouraged.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All analysis must be original work with properly cited methodologies. Using AI tools for data analysis is permitted if the student can explain every step and justify every conclusion. Fabricating or manipulating data is treated as academic fraud.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students must have Python 3.8+ with pandas, numpy, matplotlib, scipy, scikit-learn, and xgboost installed. Jupyter Notebook or Google Colab is recommended. Access to a spreadsheet application is required.
